title: Alnus rugosa
intro: Alnus rugosa, also known as the American green alder, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the birch family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, fight infections, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Alnus rugosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Alnus rugosa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, such as respiratory issues and digestive problems.

uses: Alnus rugosa is a species of plant with traditional medicinal uses. Its bark, leaves, and roots have been used in herbal medicine for various purposes, including treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
